model.env_var_note: Per Codex 2026-05-13 F2 [medium]: the shim reads CILOGBENCH_OPENAI_MODEL with the default 'gpt-5-mini'. When validating cache hits, tools/run_diagnosis.py uses os.environ[env_var_name] if set, else model_name. This makes documented env overrides (e.g. CILOGBENCH_OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-4o) idempotent: cached rows under that env value continue to match instead of being silently rejected.
model.base_url_note: Per Codex 2026-05-17 F2 [medium]: declare the canonical OpenAI endpoint so the cache key reflects effective backend identity (not the empty-string raw env value). tools/run_diagnosis.py:build_shim_env injects this default into CILOGBENCH_OPENAI_BASE_URL when the user hasn't set the env var; cache_key_env_values then reads the effective shim env (with the default already filled in) so an unset-default run and an explicit-default run produce the SAME cache key. tools/run_diagnosis.py:cache_hit_is_acceptable also validates the cached row's metadata.model_info.base_url against this effective URL on every cache hit.
model.model_alias_note: gpt-5-mini is an OpenAI alias that resolves to a dated snapshot at request time. The actual snapshot ID returned by the API is persisted in each diagnosis row's metadata.model_info.resolved_model, so a future re-run can verify whether the snapshot has changed underneath the alias.
model.temperature_note: gpt-5-mini is a reasoning-class model: temperature/top_p are ignored by the API. The shim does not send them.
model.max_completion_tokens_note: OpenAI's reasoning models use `max_completion_tokens` (capped here at 4096) instead of `max_tokens`. Reasoning tokens are not counted against this cap by the API.
model.json_mode_note: JSON-only output is requested via the system prompt + user message ('STRICT JSON ... No prose, no code fences, JSON only'). The shim's `parse_diagnosis_json` strips fence markers if any leak through.

context_policy.max_chars_note: Same cap as the Claude shim for cross-debugger comparability — contexts >480000 chars cause the shim to exit 1 with `unsupported_context_too_large` (post-Codex-2026-05-11 F1 fix; pre-fix the shim exited 0 with a placeholder body).

cache_key_env_note: Per Codex 2026-05-12 F2 [high]: these env vars override the shim's resolved model and endpoint. tools/run_diagnosis.py folds their values into the diagnosis cache_key so a re-run with a different CILOGBENCH_OPENAI_MODEL or CILOGBENCH_OPENAI_BASE_URL does not silently replay a stale row from a different backend. Adding/removing entries invalidates all existing v3 cache entries by design — the original §3i run pre-dates this opt-in and was re-run on 2026-05-13 to populate resolved_model from live API responses.
_doc: Added 2026-05-11 per Codex adversarial review on commit 772520d. The §3i cross-family validation run committed before this file existed used the same effective settings — see model card and §3i for the run-time invariants. This config is what FUTURE re-runs must match for the §3i numbers to remain reproducible. The shim records actual model_info into each row so any divergence between this config and a future run is detectable.
